codeReview.summary: Tool-boundary validation and adversarial coverage were added for the targeted tools, but the derived path hardening does not fully meet the feature requirement to reject IDs containing '..'.

issues.description: sanitizePathComponent rejects '..' as a full segment/value but still accepts IDs that merely contain '..' (for example 'safe..id' and 'feat..id'). The feature spec requires getSessionPath/getFeatureDocPath to refuse inputs containing '..'. Reproduced by evaluating getSessionPath('/tmp/wt', 'safe..id') and getFeatureDocPath('/tmp/wt', 'safe-session', 'feat..id'), both of which returned paths instead of throwing InvalidFlowPathInputError.

Adversarial path-builder tests cover '..', traversal, absolute, and separator inputs, but do not include the explicit '..' substring case (for example 'safe..id') required by the feature description's 'containing ..' wording.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The core-worker skill requires an explicit red test run before implementation, but this feature was implemented before any recorded failing run. Clarify whether red-first is mandatory or optional for small schema-driven hardening tasks to avoid inconsistent worker behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback reports followedProcedure=false for step 3; transcript skeleton shows new tests run after ApplyPatch edits and no earlier failing invocation of those test files.

summary: Fail: one blocking gap remains in derived path hardening. Inputs containing '..' as a substring are still accepted by path builders, which does not fully satisfy the stated hardening requirement.
